reasoning: ON is a cyclical play that's near the bottom of its cycle. The forward guidance (first YoY growth) is the signal that the worst may be over. But it needs to prove it in May earnings. At 1.4% of portfolio and 8% gain, this is a small bet on semiconductor recovery. Hold and let the cycle turn. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Add after May earnings if YoY growth confirmed. Exit if breaks below $50 (near 200-day MA).
entryConditions: Add after May earnings if YoY growth confirmed
bullCase: ON Semiconductor is at a cyclical inflection point. Q1 2026 revenue $1.44-1.54B guidance is the first expected YoY growth since the downturn. P/E 22.7x forward is fair to cheap vs sector 25-30x. $2.15B cash, $1.4B FCF, active buybacks. Analyst Buy consensus, avg target $65-68 (10-15% upside). Position up 8%.
bearCase: Revenue missed ($1.53B vs $1.58B), down 11.2% YoY. Gross margin compressing (~36% vs 40%). Non-GAAP EPS beat but GAAP EPS missed badly. Insider selling. Stock needs to prove recovery in May 2026 earnings. Semis facing broad rotation.
